growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Ren et al. Conformation and dynamic interactions of the multipartite genome in Agrobacterium tumefaciens. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2115854119. [PMC8833148]
growth_records.snippet: Cells were grown as specified in defined minimal medium (ATGN)... or LB broth at 30 °C with aeration. The doubling time of wild-type is 202 min ± 7 min (mean ± SD) in ATGN (n = 3) and 89 min ± 2 min (mean ± SD) in LB
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported directly for A. tumefaciens C58 wild-type grown exponentially in ATGN minimal medium at 30C with aeration (202 min ± 7 min, n=3). Converted: doubling_time_h = 202/60 = 3.37 h; growth_rate_per_h = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/3.37 = 0.206 h^-1.

growth_records.cite: Ren et al. Conformation and dynamic interactions of the multipartite genome in Agrobacterium tumefaciens. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2115854119. [PMC8833148]
growth_records.snippet: The doubling time of wild-type is 202 min ± 7 min (mean ± SD) in ATGN (n = 3) and 89 min ± 2 min (mean ± SD) in LB (n = 3).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported directly for A. tumefaciens C58 wild-type grown exponentially in LB broth at 30C with aeration (89 min ± 2 min, n=3). Converted: doubling_time_h = 89/60 = 1.48 h; growth_rate_per_h = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/1.48 = 0.467 h^-1.

growth_records.strain: C58F and C58F-Cir (single-chromosome derivatives: FL = natural fused linear chromosome; FCir = circularized fused chromosome)
growth_records.cite: Aliu et al. Chromosome architecture affects virulence and competitiveness in Agrobacterium tumefaciens C58. Science Advances (2025). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.adx7408. [PMC12494015]
growth_records.snippet: single-chromosome strains (FL and FCir) exhibiting a 20 to 30% faster growth than WT and Cir strains (1.47 to 1.48 hours versus 1.82 to 2.08 hours; Fig. 3A)
growth_records.notes: Reported doubling times for the two single-chromosome strains (FL, FCir) ranged 1.47-1.48 h in YEP broth (28°C, shaking, N=12 biological replicates); doubling_time_h recorded as the midpoint 1.475 h. growth_rate_per_h = ln2/1.475 = 0.47 h-1 (calculated). Paper does not report which of the two strains had which exact value within the range.

growth_records.strain: C58 WT and C58-Cir (bipartite/two-chromosome derivatives: WT = wild-type circular C1 + linear C2; Cir = C2 circularized)
growth_records.cite: Aliu et al. Chromosome architecture affects virulence and competitiveness in Agrobacterium tumefaciens C58. Science Advances (2025). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.adx7408. [PMC12494015]
growth_records.snippet: single-chromosome strains (FL and FCir) exhibiting a 20 to 30% faster growth than WT and Cir strains (1.47 to 1.48 hours versus 1.82 to 2.08 hours; Fig. 3A)
growth_records.notes: Reported doubling times for the two bipartite-chromosome strains (WT, Cir) ranged 1.82-2.08 h in YEP broth (28°C, shaking, N=12 biological replicates); doubling_time_h recorded as the midpoint 1.95 h. growth_rate_per_h = ln2/1.95 = 0.355 h-1 (calculated). Paper does not report which of the two strains had which exact value within the range.

up.units: not reported (concentration vs time shown only in Fig. 1A; no numeric qS given in text)
up.method: OD600 growth curve + SQ concentration ([SQ]) time course (reducing sugar / HPLC assay)

sec.units: not reported (Delta[sulfite] vs time shown only in Fig. 1A; slowly autooxidizes to sulfate)
sec.method: colorimetric fuchsin sulfite assay + turbidometric sulfate assay on culture supernatant

sec.units: not reported (transient signal, exponential phase; disappears at stationary phase)
sec.method: 13C NMR of supernatant from cells cultured on 13C-SQ (SI Appendix Fig. S2)

growth_records.cite: Mahima Sharma et al. Oxidative desulfurization pathway for complete catabolism of sulfoquinovose by bacteria. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2116022119. [PMC8795539]
growth_records.snippet: Cultures of A. tumefaciens C58 were grown in a phosphate-buffered mineral salts media (M9, pH 7.2), with glucose or SQ (10 mM) as the sole carbon source. Cultures were incubated at 30 C (250 rpm)... SQ consumption is coincident with product
growth_records.notes: Genuine experimental prokaryote growth condition: A. tumefaciens C58 grows robustly on 10 mM SQ as sole carbon source (M9, pH 7.2, 30 C, 250 rpm, batch, aerobic). NO numeric physiological rates are reported in the text: growth (OD600), SQ consumption ([SQ]) and sulfite production (Delta[sulfite]) are plotted vs time only in Fig. 1A, so no mu, doubling time, qS or qP could be computed without figure-digitized values (not permitted). SQ uptake is qualitative (coincident with sulfite release); sulfite is the excreted product (autooxidizes to sulfate); transient 13C-bicarbonate detected during exponential phase by 13C NMR on 13C-SQ. M9 salt composition not enumerated in this paper (only carbon source conc 10 mM stated), hence medium_composition_stated=false. A. tumefaciens also grew on sulfoquinovosyl glycerol (SQGro) and other sulfoquinovosides (Fig. S3) but no separate quantitative conditions given; no growth on PIPES, cysteic acid, or methanesulfonic acid. This is a biochemistry/structural paper (enzyme kinetics, crystallography) not a GEM/13C-MFA physiology study.

up.method: OD600 growth curve (sole carbon source); no numeric uptake rate given

growth_records.cite: Mahima Sharma et al. Oxidative desulfurization pathway for complete catabolism of sulfoquinovose by bacteria. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2116022119. [PMC8795539]
growth_records.snippet: Cultures of A. tumefaciens C58 were grown in a phosphate-buffered mineral salts media (M9, pH 7.2), with glucose or SQ (10 mM) as the sole carbon source. Cultures were incubated at 30 C (250 rpm), with adaptation and robust growth observed
growth_records.notes: Glucose-grown control condition (M9, pH 7.2, 30 C, 250 rpm, batch, aerobic, 10 mM glucose sole carbon). Serves as the reference for SQ-vs-glucose comparative proteomics (Fig. 1B, midlog phase). No growth rate or uptake/secretion rate quantified numerically. M9 salt composition not enumerated (only carbon-source conc stated), so medium_composition_stated=false.
